channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Lithuanians vote in parliamentary election with many looking for change despite a strong economy
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Lithuanians voted Sunday in the first round of parliamentary elections that could lead to the center-right governing coalition being replaced by the opposition Social Democrats and smaller center-left parties.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Macao's former top judge is elected as the Chinese casino hub's first leader born in mainland China
item.description: MACAO (AP) — Macao’s former top judge, Sam Hou Fai, was chosen as the Chinese casino hub’ s next leader in a largely ceremonial election on Sunday, setting him up to become the city’s first chief executive born in mainland China.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Palestinians in northern Gaza described heavy Israeli bombardment Saturday in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people, as Israel warned people there and in southern Lebanon to get out of the way of offensives against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Palestinian officials say 22 killed in Israeli airstrikes
item.description: The Israeli military on Saturday renewed its orders for Palestinians in the northern Gaza Strip to leave their homes and shelters as troops continue a weeklong offensive against militants.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ex-Scottish First Minister Alex Salmond, who nearly got Scotland's independence from UK, dies at 69
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Alex Salmond, the former first minister of Scotland who for decades championed Scotland’s independence from the U.K. and nearly accomplished it, has died. He was 69.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland's leader plans to suspend the right to asylum as country faces pressure on Belarus border
item.description: Poland’s leader says he plans to temporarily suspend the right to asylum as part of a new migration policy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A-bomb survivors use Nobel Peace Prize to share anti-nuke message with the young
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — The recipient of this year's Nobel Peace Prize is a fast-dwindling group of atomic bomb survivors who are facing down the shrinking time they have left to convey the firsthand horror they witnessed 79 years ago. Nihon Hidankyo, the Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hindus in Bangladesh celebrate their largest festival under tight security following attacks
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Schoolteacher Supriya Sarker is glad to celebrate Bangladesh's largest Hindu festival of Durga Puja but feels the festivities would be more jubilant without the fear and violence that overshadow this year's event.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM and Al EMRUN GARJON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Drought is parching the world's largest man-made lake, stripping Zambia of its electricity
item.description: Zambia’s worst electricity blackouts in memory have been caused by a severe drought in the region that has left the critical Kariba dam with insufficient water to run its hydroelectric turbines.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Zimba, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers in separate strikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — An Israeli airstrike killed two Lebanese soldiers and wounded three on Friday, Lebanon's military said, just hours after the Israeli military fired on the headquarters of U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon, injuring two of them for the second day in a row.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Boris Johnson says Biden told him his family origins 'were not really Irish at all'
item.description: Boris Johnson, former United Kingdom prime minister, said President Biden told him he was not Irish, according to his new memoir "Unleashed," which was published Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize given to Japanese organization Nihon Hidankyo for its work against nuclear weapons
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize was awarded Friday to Nihon Hidankyo, a Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, for its activism against nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER and ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Stellantis CEO under fire from Italian lawmakers as the group grapples with financial troubles
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italian lawmakers on Friday questioned the head of Stellantis, the world's fourth largest automaker, over the group’s strategy, one day after it announced a management shakeup in an effort to revive sales.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New arrests revive concern that nonviolent political protesters in Myanmar may be tortured
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Concern was rising on Friday that two nonviolent activists opposed to military rule in Myanmar are at high risk of torture after being arrested in raids this week in Yangon, the country’s biggest city.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bolton: Odds that Trump would withdraw from NATO very high
item.description: Former White House national security adviser John Bolton warned that former President Trump is likely to withdraw from NATO if reelected in November.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Russian and Iranian presidents meet as concerns grow over escalating Middle East attacks
item.description: ASHGABAT, Turkmenistan (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin met Iran’s president on Friday, at a time when Tehran is supplying weapons for Moscow’s war in Ukraine and concerns are growing over escalating attacks between Israel and Iran and its militant allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia launches 4th aerial attack in a week against Ukraine's grain-exporting Odesa region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A nighttime Russian missile strike on Odesa killed at least four people including a 16-year-old girl, regional authorities said Friday, in the latest in a series of attacks this week on the southern Ukrainian region that are likely intended to disrupt the country’s grain exports
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gender of female captain not to blame for sinking of $61M navy ship: New Zealand defense minister
item.description: New Zealand's Defense Minister has rebukes claims that the appointment of a female to captain a $61 million navy ship ultimately led to its sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN: Israeli military fired on peacekeepers in southern Lebanon
item.description: The United Nations on Thursday accused the Israeli military of firing on peacekeeper missions in southern Lebanon as Israel conducts a cross-border operation inside the country.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel continues to weigh options, timing on Iran strike following Biden-Netanyahu call
item.description: President Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held a call that in part focused on Iran's recent missile attack on the Jewish state, and reportedly how Israel intends to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archaeologists unearth unexpected find inside a tomb likely belonging to a Roman gladiator
item.description: In Turkey, archaeologists unearthed a tomb seemingly belonging to a Roman gladiator of the third century B.C., but were surprised to find the remains of 12 individuals inside.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian woman charged with killing 3 people in 3 days, labeled a serial killer
item.description: A Canadian woman allegedly killed three people in separate incidents over three days in and around Toronto, authorities said last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli defense minister warns an attack on Iran would be 'lethal' and 'surprising'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's defense minister warned on Wednesday that his country's retaliation for a recent Iranian missile attack will be “lethal” and “surprising,” while the Israeli military pushed ahead with a large-scale operation in northern Gaza and a ground offensive in Lebanon against Hezboll
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: To cope with 'confrontational hysteria' by U.S. and South Korean forces, North Korea said it will block its border with South Korea permanently and will cut off railways and roads to the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: The history of the Hoxne Hoard, the largest collection of Roman treasure found in Britain
item.description: The Hoxne Hoard is one of the most significant archaeological finds out of Britain. The hoard is mostly made of gold and silver coins, although other unique objects add to its value.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk's X to be reinstated in Brazil after complying with Supreme Court demands
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — The Brazilian Supreme Court's Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Tuesday authorized the restoration of social media platform X´s service in Brazil, over a month after its nationwide shutdown, according to a court document that was made public. Elon Musk’s X was blocked on Aug.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu confirms Nasrallah's replacement dead: ‘Thousands of terrorists’ killed
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed the death of the Hezbollah leader slated to replace Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah and warned the Lebanese people to oust the terrorist group or endure more war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hungary’s Orban interrupted, accused of 'selling out' country to Russia, China during EU news conference
item.description: Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban was interrupted at a news conference by an activist and municipal councilor from the opposition Democratic Coalition party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea’s Kim Jong Un pushes goal to become nuclear powerhouse in warning to South Korea
item.description: Kim Jong Un issued his second ominous message, saying he plans to make Pyongyang a top nuclear player and expand its capabilities in face of South Korea-U.S. partnership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The 4,000 items in the Staffordshire Hoard make it the largest collection in history of its kind
item.description: The Staffordshire Hoard is made of thousands of items. It was discovered by a farmer in 2009 and is the largest collection of its kind.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The world's rivers faced the driest year in three decades in 2023, the UN weather agency says
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — The U.N. weather agency is reporting that 2023 was the driest year in more than three decades for the world's rivers, as the record-hot year underpinned a drying up of water flows and contributed to prolonged droughts in some places.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Abigail Edan's aunt says 'we need a deal' on Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: "I say this to the Palestinian people, and I say this to the Israelis Diplomacy now is what we need," Naftali said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: New report finds nearly 200% increase in antisemitic incidents in US since Oct. 7 Hamas terror massacre
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League recorded around 10,000 antisemitic incidents since Oct. 7, marking the largest year-to-year increase since the group started recording such incidents in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
